benchmark_honesty_caveat: Autonomous second-round repair-context selector output. Hidden aligned target Lean declarations, statements, names, and proofs are not included. Mathlib signatures here are included only after Lean #check hydration.

To drop zeros when converting back from NPartition to Nat.Partition.

missing_or_uncertain_context.item: Nat.Partition has no length field; we need to define length as Multiset.card of parts. The source says 'partitions of length ≤ N', so we need a predicate on Nat.Partition that its length (number of parts) is ≤ N. This can be defined as Multiset.card p.parts ≤ N. The visible context does not provide this predicate, but it can be defined locally.

The antitone condition for the padded NPartition requires that the original partition parts are sorted decreasingly. Nat.Partition.parts is a Multiset, not necessarily sorted. The source assumes partitions are written as weakly decreasing tuples. We need to use Multiset.sort to get a sorted list. The antitone proof then follows from the sortedness of the sorted list. This is doable with Multiset.sort_sorted and the fact that the sorted list is decreasing.

The bijection should be an Equiv between the subtype of Nat.Partition n with length ≤ N and NPartition N, not a Function.Bijective on a sigma type. The sigma type approach was incorrect. We need to define the set of partitions of length ≤ N as a subtype.

proof_strategy_notes.failure_analysis: The previous attempt tried to define fromPartition using Multiset.sort and toNPartition returning a sigma type, but could not prove the bijection. The main obstacles are: (1) Nat.Partition has no length field; (2) the antitone proof in fromPartition is incomplete; (3) toNPartition does not enforce the length ≤ N condition; (4) the bijection statement uses Function.Bijective on a sigma type which is not the intended set-level bijection. The visible context provides NPartition (with parts: Fin N → ℕ and antitone), Nat.Partition (with parts: Multiset ℕ, parts_pos, parts_sum), and the local predecessor toPartition (NPartition → Nat.Partition μ.size). A better route: define fromPartition by padding a Nat.Partition with zeros to length N using an explicit list, then prove antitone by using the sortedness of the partition parts. Define toNPartition as the inverse by taking the NPartition parts, filtering out zeros, and constructing a Nat.Partition (reusing the existing toPartition). Then prove the bijection as an Equiv (or two inverse functions) between the set of partitions with length ≤ N and NPartition N.
proof_strategy_notes.proof_strategy_note: Define lengthLE as a predicate. Define fromPartition' by taking the sorted list of parts (using Multiset.sort (· ≥ ·) p.parts), then for each i:Fin N, if i < length of sorted list, take the i-th element; else 0. Prove antitone using Multiset.sort_sorted. Define toNPartition' by reusing the existing toPartition to get a Nat.Partition μ.size, then prove that its length (Multiset.card of parts) ≤ N because the original NPartition has at most N nonzero entries (since it has N entries total). Finally, prove the two maps are inverse using extensionality lemmas.

units.repair_strategy_note: Define lengthLE as a predicate. Define fromPartition' by taking the sorted list of parts (using Multiset.sort (· ≥ ·) p.parts), then for each i:Fin N, if i < length of sorted list, take the i-th element; else 0. Prove antitone using Multiset.sort_sorted. Define toNPartition' by reusing the existing toPartition to get a Nat.Partition μ.size, then prove that its length (Multiset.card of parts) ≤ N because the original NPartition has at most N nonzero entries (since it has N entries total). Finally, prove the two maps are inverse using extensionality lemmas.

same_unit_helper_plan.statement_sketch: def lengthLE (n N : ℕ) (p : Nat.Partition n) : Prop := Multiset.card p.parts ≤ N
same_unit_helper_plan.why_needed: Predicate for partitions of length ≤ N.

same_unit_helper_plan.statement_sketch:
def fromPartition' {n N : ℕ} (p : Nat.Partition n) (h : lengthLE n N p) : NPartition N where
  parts i := ... (pad sorted list with zeros)
  antitone := ... (using sortedness of sorted list)
def toNPartition' {N : ℕ} (μ : NPartition N) : Σ n, { p : Nat.Partition n // lengthLE n N p } := ... (reuse toPartition, then prove length condition)
same_unit_helper_plan.why_needed: Inverse direction, returning a partition with length ≤ N.

same_unit_helper_plan.statement_sketch: theorem bijection_equiv {n N : ℕ} : (Σ' (p : Nat.Partition n), lengthLE n N p) ≃ NPartition N := ...
